title: The confident off-by-one
summary: The AI wrote a paginator. It looks right. Did you check the edges?
task: Add a helper that returns the last page index (0-based) for a list, given a page size.

hiddenIssues.title: Off-by-one when total is an exact multiple of pageSize
hiddenIssues.explanation: For total=20, pageSize=10 this returns 2, but the last valid 0-based page index is 1. A boundary test (exact multiple, and total=0) catches it. Fix: Math.max(0, Math.ceil(total / pageSize) - 1).

debrief: Type-checking and linting both pass — the code is valid, just wrong. Only a test that probes the boundary (exact multiples, empty input) catches an off-by-one. This is the classic AI trap: confident, clean, and subtly incorrect. Running everything 'to be safe' wastes effort; the calibrated move is a boundary test.
